data.title: Airbus's plane deliveries are said to have jumped past 80 in November
data.description:
• Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) in November delivered more than 80 commercial planes to customers, the highest November tally since 2018, Reuters reported on Monday, citing industry sources.
  • The deliveries show progress toward the planemaker’s year-end goal of about 770 planes, but many analysts don’t expect Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) to hit that target.
  • Its year-to-date total is about 640 deliveries. Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) in December last year delivered 112 planes.
  • The company for the past few years has grappled with shortages of parts and labor as demand for air travel recovered after the pandemic.
  • The company will report its official November number on December 6.

data.title: Sleep Number holder Stadium Capital nominates four for board
data.description:
Sleep Number's (SNBR) largest shareholder Stadium Capital nominated four directors amid a proxy fight with the mattress retailer.

Stadium Capital, which is Sleep Number's (SNBR) largest holder with an 11.7% stake, nominated Patrick A. Hopf, Jeffrey T. Jackson, Jessica M. Prager and Kevin Baker to the company's board, according to a letter sent to SBNR's board on Monday.

The letter comes after Stadium a week ago threatened a new proxy fight with the mattress retailer. Stadium said it wanted an executive chairman appointed to run the company as CEO Shelly Ibach exits the company, which Sleep Number (SNBR) announced late last month.

"We did not come to this decision lightly, as we strongly prefer private engagement with the management teams and boards of our portfolio companies on ideas for value creation and improved governance," Stadium Capital wrote in the letter.

Sleep Number (SNBR) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha email request for comment.

Stadium last September threatened a proxy fight and then came to an agreement with Sleep Number (SNBR) to add two directors to the board.

Shares of Sleep Number (SNBR) fell 0.8% on Monday.

data.title: Airline stocks look to take off with holiday travel: See 10 of SA’s top quant picks
data.description:
As the holiday season approaches, many investors are forecasting an uptick in consumer travel, with individuals heading to visit family and friends or taking year-end vacations—trends that signal a possible busy seasonal travel period.

As a result, commercial airline stocks are drawing increased attention as the potential holiday travel surge gets underway. In response, Seeking Alpha has compiled a list of the top 10 U.S. airline stocks based on its quantitative ratings for investors. See the list below:

No. 10: Southwest Airlines Co (LUV), quant rating of 3.29.

No. 9: Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT), quant rating of 3.36.

No. 8: Sun Country Airlines Holdings (SNCY), quant rating of 4.43.

No. 7: Joby Aviation (JOBY), quant rating of 4.48.

No. 6: Delta Air Lines (DAL), quant rating of 4.64.

No. 5: American Airlines Group (AAL), quant rating of 4.72.

No. 4: Frontier Group Holdings (ULCC), quant rating of 4.81.

No. 3: Alaska Group Holdings (ALK), quant rating of 4.82.

No. 2: SkyWest (SKYW), quant rating of 4.94.

No. 1: United Airlines Holdings (UAL), quant rating of 4.97.

Year-to-date price action: LUV +12.1%, ALGT -0.9%, SNCY -8.5%, JOBY +34.6%, DAL +58.6%, AAL +5.7%, ULCC +6.8%, ALK +34.6%, SKYW +119.8%, and UAL +134.7%.

Moreover, for a more diversified approach towards the commercial airline space investors can further analyze the U.S. Global Jets ETF (NYSEARCA:JETS) which is a fund that provides exposure to a group of 57 aviation holdings.

data.title: U.S. manufacturing PMI rises more than expected in November
data.description: • S&P Global U.S. Manufacturing PMI: 49.7 vs. 48.8 consensus, Nov. flash number of 48.8 and 48.5 in October.

data.title: Fastly jumps, receives upgrade with Akamai following Edgio auction: Oppenheimer
data.description:
Fastly (NYSE:FSLY) and Akamai (NASDAQ:AKAM) both made gains during early Monday trading due in part to the recent bankruptcy of Edgio (OTC:EGIOQ), as consolidation in the content delivery network sector serves as a positive catalyst.

Fastly was upgraded to Outperform from Perform and received a $12 price target by Oppenheimer. Shares closed 7% higher on Friday and were up more than 10% during early market action on Monday. Shares collapsed in February 2024, and are recently showing signs of life, gaining 12% over the past month.

"FSLY should benefit from Edgio's bankruptcy at the same time that Internet volumes and pricing are improving," said Oppenheimer analyst Tim Horan, in a Monday note. "AKAM recently received court approval over the purchase of approximately 1/3 of Edgio's customer contracts, or $100M, and we expect FSLY to capture $40M of revenue."

"We have increased our FSLY estimates on this and our expectations for strong demand for its edge compute and security products driven by new AI applications," Horan added. "Regardless, over 75% of its revenue is CDN related and the industry has now consolidated down to a three player market from six in 15 months with FSLY the premium provider."

Akamai was the other benefactor of Edgio's bankruptcy. Oppenheimer upgraded its rating to Outperform from Perform and set a $120 price target on the stock. Akamai ticked up 2% during Monday morning trading.

In addition to acquiring select assets from Edgio during its bankruptcy auction, Akamai "has also now built out a solid cloud compute and edge compute platform, and the latter will benefit from demand for AI inferencing and AI cyberattacks," Horan noted.

Similar to Fastly, Akamai's shares dropped precipitously during mid-February, but recently started clawing their way back up.

"AKAM is paying ~$150M for Edgio, including transition costs," Horan said. "We conservatively estimate associated revenues have ~80% gross margins and EBITDA margins in the 60% range, and higher than this next year as the company has low asset utilization. So we are probably looking at a 2-year payback period, with a certainty that the Edgio infrastructure is being shut down."

data.title: Genius Group increases Bitcoin treasury to 172 Bitcoin
data.description:
• Genius Group Limited (NYSE:GNS) announced an increase in its Bitcoin purchases for its Bitcoin Treasury by an additional $1.8M. 
  • The company now holds 172 Bitcoin, acquired for a total of $15.8M, at an average net price of $92,006 per Bitcoin.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kulicke and Soffa Industries completes $800M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Kulicke and Soffa Industries (NASDAQ:KLIC) completed its $800M share repurchase program, and has expanded its shareholder return activities with a new $300M stock buyback program.
  • KLIC shares were trading -1.80% Monday pre-market at $47.55.
  • The semiconductor and electronics assembly solutions company repurchased ~22.7M shares at about $35.25 per share.
  • Additionally, the company has again expanded its shareholder return activities with a new $300M stock buyback program.
  • Kulicke and Soffa may repurchase shares of common stock through open market and privately negotiated transactions at prices deemed appropriate by the management.
  • The company said it has deployed $900M to repurchase a total of 30.9M shares at an average price of ~$29.12 per share since the announcement of its initial repurchase program on Aug. 27, 2014.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Psychemedics announces 1-for-5,000 reverse and forward stock split
data.description:
• Psychemedics Corporation (NASDAQ:PMD) approved a 1-for-5,000 reverse stock split of its common stock followed immediately by a 5,000-for-1 forward stock split of its common stock to be effective at 5:01 p.m. and 5:02 p.m.,ET, respectively, on Tuesday, December 3, 2024.
  • Beginning with the opening of trading on Wednesday, December 4, 2024, the company’s common stock will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market on a post-Forward Stock Split basis under the existing symbol “PMD”.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gelteq’s largest U.S. customer increases initial order by 50%
data.description:
• Gelteq Limited (NASDAQ:GELS) said on Monday that its largest U.S. customer, Healthy Extracts (OTCQB:HYEX), has increased its initial purchase order of Gelteq collagen and Mynus sugar blocker gels by 50%, citing high demand and popularity of the products.
  • Healthy Extracts’ platform acquires, develops, patents, markets, and distributes plant-based nutraceuticals that target select high-growth categories within the nutraceuticals market.
  • Production of Healthy Extracts’ order is underway, with shipment expected in January 2025.
  • Gelteq Limited (GELS) shares were up more than 10% in pre-market trading on Monday.

data.title: CEMATRIX announces Randy Boomhour as president and CEO
data.description:
• CEMATRIX Corporation (OTCQB:CTXXF) announced that Randy Boomhour has been named president and chief executive officer effective November 29, 2024.
  • Boomhour joined CEMATRIX in 2020 as finance chief and was promoted to chief operating officer in 2024.
  • Source: Press Release
